repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Problem:  'inccommand' preview is not executed after setcmdline(),
          and as a result cmdline_show event is emitted when redrawing
          is not allowed (5b6477be).
Solution: Call command_line_changed() when ccline.cmdbuff_replaced is
          set (by setcmdline()).

Fix #38764

comment_data.body: The `may_trigger_cursormovedc()` below needs this as well?
This guard is to prevent double `CmdlineChanged` events, `set_cmdline_str()` only does `CmdlineChanged`, not `CursorMovedC`, so no I don't think so.
I don't think `cmdbuff_replaced = false` should be moved here. Resetting it here is too early and does not end the wildmenu when it should. The original position is better.
No this is earlier. The line above is executed in the next main loop iteration, while this line is executed in the current one.
Again this shouldn't be reset here, otherwise `command_line_end_wildmenu()` won't be called in the next main loop iteration.

title: fix(cmdline): 'inccommand' preview after setcmdline()
